mn150:1.2.pli: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu cārikaṁ caramāno mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ yena nagaravindaṁ nāma kosalānaṁ brāhmaṇānaṁ gāmo tadavasari.
mn150:1.2.eng: At one time the Buddha was wandering in the land of the Kosalans together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants when he arrived at a village of the Kosalan brahmins named Nagaravinda.
mn150:1.2.comm: We travel with the Buddha to several “villages of the Kosalan brahmins”, but this is the only recorded visit to Nagaravinda. The name seems to mean “mountain-lotus” (naga = “mountain”, aravinda = “lotus”). Sāvatthī lies only fifty kilometres south of the Himalayan foothills.

mn150:2.1.eng: The brahmins and householders of Nagaravinda heard,

mn150:2.2.pli: “samaṇo khalu, bho, gotamo sakyaputto sakyakulā pabbajito kosalesu cārikaṁ caramāno mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ nagaravindaṁ anuppatto.
mn150:2.2.eng: “It seems the ascetic Gotama—a Sakyan, gone forth from a Sakyan family—while wandering in the land of the Kosalans has arrived at Nagaravinda, together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants.

mn150:2.3.pli: Taṁ kho pana bhavantaṁ gotamaṁ evaṁ kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggato:

mn150:2.4.pli: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti.
mn150:2.4.eng: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’

mn150:2.5.pli: So imaṁ lokaṁ sadevakaṁ samārakaṁ sabrahmakaṁ sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiṁ pajaṁ sadevamanussaṁ sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā pavedeti.
mn150:2.5.eng: He has realized with his own insight this world—with its gods, Māras, and divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, gods and humans—and he makes it known to others.

mn150:2.6.pli: So dhammaṁ deseti ādikalyāṇaṁ majjhekalyāṇaṁ pariyosānakalyāṇaṁ sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ, kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ pakāseti.
mn150:2.6.eng: He proclaims a teaching that is good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased. And he reveals a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure.

mn150:2.7.pli: Sādhu kho pana tathārūpānaṁ arahataṁ dassanaṁ hotī”ti.
mn150:2.7.eng: It’s good to see such perfected ones.”

mn150:3.1.pli: Atha kho nagaravindeyyakā brāhmaṇagahapatikā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā appekacce bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Appekacce bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu; sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Appekacce yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Appekacce bhagavato santike nāmagottaṁ sāvetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Appekacce tuṇhībhūtā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho nagaravindeyyake brāhmaṇagahapatike bhagavā etadavoca:
mn150:3.1.eng: Then the brahmins and householders of Nagaravinda went up to the Buddha. Before sitting down to one side, some bowed, some exchanged greetings and polite conversation, some held up their joined palms toward the Buddha, some announced their name and clan, while some kept silent. The Buddha said to them:

mn150:4.1.pli: “Sace vo, gahapatayo, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā evaṁ puccheyyuṁ:
mn150:4.1.eng: “Householders, if wanderers who follow another religion were to ask you:
mn150:4.1.comm: This is an unusual framing, as usually the Buddha refers to wanderers of another religion when speaking with mendicants. The parallel simply says “other people” (SA 280 at T ii 76c8). Again, the fact that the villagers have diverse responses to the Buddha shows that they are not all Buddhists, and hence it would be out of character for the Buddha to assume otherwise, as the teaching here implies. Further, the Buddha’s statement here reads as if it were an answer to a question on how to discern which of the many different ascetics were worthy of respect. It seems to me likely that the narrative has become a little confused.

mn150:4.2.pli: ‘kathaṁbhūtā, gahapatayo, samaṇabrāhmaṇā na sakkātabbā na garukātabbā na mānetabbā na pūjetabbā’ti?
mn150:4.2.eng: ‘What kind of ascetic or brahmin doesn’t deserve honor, respect, reverence, and veneration?’

mn150:4.3.pli: Evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, gahapatayo, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyātha:

mn150:4.4.pli: ‘ye te samaṇabrāhmaṇā cakkhuviññeyyesu rūpesu avītarāgā avītadosā avītamohā, ajjhattaṁ avūpasantacittā, samavisamaṁ caranti kāyena vācāya manasā, evarūpā samaṇabrāhmaṇā na sakkātabbā na garukātabbā na mānetabbā na pūjetabbā.
mn150:4.4.eng: ‘There are ascetics and brahmins who are not free of greed, hate, and delusion for sights known by the eye, who are not peaceful inside, and who conduct themselves badly among the good by way of body, speech, and mind. They don’t deserve honor, respect, reverence, and veneration.
mn150:4.4.comm: The idiom samavisamaṁ caranti means “walk roughly in the smooth”, in other words, “conduct themselves badly among the good”, in contrast with those who “walk smoothly in the rough” (caranti visame samaṁ, SN 1.7:3.4, SN 2.6:8.6).

mn150:4.6.pli: Mayampi hi cakkhuviññeyyesu rūpesu avītarāgā avītadosā avītamohā, ajjhattaṁ avūpasantacittā, samavisamaṁ carāma kāyena vācāya manasā, tesaṁ no samacariyampi hetaṁ uttari apassataṁ.
mn150:4.6.eng: Because we ourselves are not free of these things, so we do not see that they have any higher good conduct than us.

mn150:4.7.pli: Tasmā te bhonto samaṇabrāhmaṇā na sakkātabbā na garukātabbā na mānetabbā na pūjetabbā.
mn150:4.7.eng: That’s why they don’t deserve honor, respect, reverence, and veneration.

mn150:4.8.eng: There are ascetics and brahmins who are not free of greed, hate, and delusion for sounds known by the ear …

mn150:4.12.pli: manoviññeyyesu dhammesu avītarāgā avītadosā avītamohā, ajjhattaṁ avūpasantacittā, samavisamaṁ caranti kāyena vācāya manasā, evarūpā samaṇabrāhmaṇā na sakkātabbā na garukātabbā na mānetabbā na pūjetabbā.
mn150:4.12.eng: ideas known by the mind, who are not peaceful inside, and who conduct themselves badly among the good by way of body, speech, and mind. They don’t deserve honor, respect, reverence, and veneration.

mn150:4.14.pli: Mayampi hi manoviññeyyesu dhammesu avītarāgā avītadosā avītamohā, ajjhattaṁ avūpasantacittā, samavisamaṁ carāma kāyena vācāya manasā, tesaṁ no samacariyampi hetaṁ uttari apassataṁ.
mn150:4.14.eng: Because we ourselves are not free of these things, so we do not see that they have any higher good conduct than us.

mn150:4.15.pli: Tasmā te bhonto samaṇabrāhmaṇā na sakkātabbā na garukātabbā na mānetabbā na pūjetabbā’ti.
mn150:4.15.eng: That’s why they don’t deserve honor, respect, reverence, and veneration.’
mn150:4.15.comm: This is a much higher standard than that implied at MN 142:8.1. However, the context is different: there, it is whether offerings will be fruitful, here it is whether they are truly deserving of respect.

mn150:4.16.pli: Evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, gahapatayo, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyātha.
mn150:4.16.eng: When questioned by wanderers of other religions, that’s how you should answer them.

mn150:5.1.pli: Sace pana vo, gahapatayo, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā evaṁ puccheyyuṁ:
mn150:5.1.eng: If wanderers of other religions were to ask you:

mn150:5.2.pli: ‘kathaṁbhūtā, gahapatayo, samaṇabrāhmaṇā sakkātabbā garukātabbā mānetabbā pūjetabbā’ti?
mn150:5.2.eng: ‘What kind of ascetic or brahmin deserves honor, respect, reverence, and veneration?’

mn150:5.3.pli: Evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, gahapatayo, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyātha:

mn150:5.4.pli: ‘ye te samaṇabrāhmaṇā cakkhuviññeyyesu rūpesu vītarāgā vītadosā vītamohā, ajjhattaṁ vūpasantacittā, samacariyaṁ caranti kāyena vācāya manasā, evarūpā samaṇabrāhmaṇā sakkātabbā garukātabbā mānetabbā pūjetabbā.
mn150:5.4.eng: ‘There are ascetics and brahmins who are free of greed, hate, and delusion for sights known by the eye, who are peaceful inside, and who conduct themselves well by way of body, speech, and mind. They deserve honor, respect, reverence, and veneration.

mn150:5.6.pli: Mayampi hi cakkhuviññeyyesu rūpesu avītarāgā avītadosā avītamohā, ajjhattaṁ avūpasantacittā, samavisamaṁ carāma kāyena vācāya manasā, tesaṁ no samacariyampi hetaṁ uttari passataṁ.
mn150:5.6.eng: Because we ourselves are not free of these things, but we see that they have a higher good conduct than us.

mn150:5.7.pli: Tasmā te bhonto samaṇabrāhmaṇā sakkātabbā garukātabbā mānetabbā pūjetabbā.
mn150:5.7.eng: That’s why they deserve honor, respect, reverence, and veneration.

mn150:5.8.eng: There are ascetics and brahmins who are free of greed, hate, and delusion for sounds known by the ear …

mn150:5.12.pli: manoviññeyyesu dhammesu vītarāgā vītadosā vītamohā, ajjhattaṁ vūpasantacittā, samacariyaṁ caranti kāyena vācāya manasā, evarūpā samaṇabrāhmaṇā sakkātabbā garukātabbā mānetabbā pūjetabbā.
mn150:5.12.eng: ideas known by the mind, who are peaceful inside, and who conduct themselves well by way of body, speech, and mind. They deserve honor, respect, reverence, and veneration.

mn150:5.14.pli: Mayampi hi manoviññeyyesu dhammesu avītarāgā avītadosā avītamohā ajjhattaṁ avūpasantacittā, samavisamaṁ carāma kāyena vācāya manasā, tesaṁ no samacariyampi hetaṁ uttari passataṁ.
mn150:5.14.eng: Because we ourselves are not free of these things, but we see that they have a higher good conduct than us.

mn150:5.15.pli: Tasmā te bhonto samaṇabrāhmaṇā sakkātabbā garukātabbā mānetabbā pūjetabbā’ti.
mn150:5.15.eng: That’s why they deserve honor, respect, reverence, and veneration.

mn150:5.16.pli: Evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, gahapatayo, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyātha.
mn150:5.16.eng: When questioned by wanderers of other religions, that’s how you should answer them.

mn150:6.1.pli: Sace pana vo, gahapatayo, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā evaṁ puccheyyuṁ:
mn150:6.1.eng: If wanderers of other religions were to ask you:

mn150:6.2.pli: ‘ke panāyasmantānaṁ ākārā, ke anvayā, yena tumhe āyasmanto evaṁ vadetha?
mn150:6.2.eng: ‘But what reasons and evidence do you have regarding those venerables that justifies saying,

mn150:6.3.pli: Addhā te āyasmanto vītarāgā vā rāgavinayāya vā paṭipannā, vītadosā vā dosavinayāya vā paṭipannā, vītamohā vā mohavinayāya vā paṭipannā’ti?
mn150:6.3.eng: “Clearly those venerables are free of greed, hate, and delusion, or practicing to be free of them”?’

mn150:6.4.pli: Evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, gahapatayo, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyātha:

mn150:6.5.pli: ‘tathā hi te āyasmanto araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni paṭisevanti.
mn150:6.5.eng: ‘It’s because those venerables frequent remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.

mn150:6.6.pli: Natthi kho pana tattha tathārūpā cakkhuviññeyyā rūpā ye disvā disvā abhirameyyuṁ, natthi kho pana tattha tathārūpā sotaviññeyyā saddā ye sutvā sutvā abhirameyyuṁ, natthi kho pana tattha tathārūpā ghānaviññeyyā gandhā ye ghāyitvā ghāyitvā abhirameyyuṁ, natthi kho pana tattha tathārūpā jivhāviññeyyā rasā ye sāyitvā sāyitvā abhirameyyuṁ, natthi kho pana tattha tathārūpā kāyaviññeyyā phoṭṭhabbā ye phusitvā phusitvā abhirameyyuṁ.
mn150:6.6.eng: In such places there are no sights known by the eye to see and enjoy, there are no sounds known by the ear to hear and enjoy, no odors known by the nose to smell and enjoy, no flavors known by the tongue to taste and enjoy, and no touches known by the body to feel and enjoy.

mn150:6.7.pli: Ime kho no, āvuso, ākārā, ime anvayā, yena mayaṁ evaṁ vadema—
mn150:6.7.eng: These are the reasons and evidence that you have regarding those venerables that justifies saying,

mn150:6.8.pli: addhā te āyasmanto vītarāgā vā rāgavinayāya vā paṭipannā, vītadosā vā dosavinayāya vā paṭipannā, vītamohā vā mohavinayāya vā paṭipannā’ti.
mn150:6.8.eng: “Clearly those venerables are free of greed, hate, and delusion, or practicing to be free of them”.’

mn150:6.9.pli: Evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, gahapatayo, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyāthā”ti.
mn150:6.9.eng: When questioned by wanderers of other religions, that’s how you should answer them.”

mn150:7.1.pli: Evaṁ vutte, nagaravindeyyakā brāhmaṇagahapatikā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
mn150:7.1.eng: When he had spoken, the brahmins and householders of Nagaravinda said to the Buddha,

mn150:7.3.pli: Seyyathāpi, bho gotama, nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya, paṭicchannaṁ vā vivareyya, mūḷhassa vā maggaṁ ācikkheyya, andhakāre vā telapajjotaṁ dhāreyya: ‘cakkhumanto rūpāni dakkhantī’ti; evamevaṁ bhotā gotamena anekapariyāyena dhammo pakāsito.
mn150:7.3.eng: As if he were righting the overturned, or revealing the hidden, or pointing out the path to the lost, or lighting a lamp in the dark so people with clear eyes can see what’s there, worthy Gotama has made the Teaching clear in many ways.

mn150:7.4.pli: Ete mayaṁ bhavantaṁ gotamaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāma dhammañca bhikkhusaṅghañca.
mn150:7.4.eng: We go for refuge to the worthy Gotama, to the teaching, and to the mendicant Saṅgha.

mn150:7.5.pli: Upāsake no bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupete saraṇaṁ gate”ti.
mn150:7.5.eng: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember us as lay followers who have gone for refuge for life.”

mn151:1.2.pli: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
mn151:1.2.eng: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.

mn151:1.3.pli: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
mn151:1.3.eng: Then in the late afternoon, Sāriputta came out of retreat and went to the Buddha. He bowed and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,

mn151:2.1.pli: “Vippasannāni kho te, sāriputta, indriyāni, parisuddho chavivaṇṇo pariyodāto.
mn151:2.1.eng: “Sāriputta, your faculties are so very clear, and your complexion is pure and bright.

mn151:2.2.pli: Katamena kho tvaṁ, sāriputta, vihārena etarahi bahulaṁ viharasī”ti?
mn151:2.2.eng: What kind of meditation are you usually practicing these days?”

mn151:2.3.pli: “Suññatāvihārena kho ahaṁ, bhante, etarahi bahulaṁ viharāmī”ti.
mn151:2.3.eng: “Sir, these days I usually practice the meditation on emptiness.”
mn151:2.3.comm: See MN 121:3.3 and note. This discourse offers a different perspective on this meditation.

mn151:2.5.pli: Mahāpurisavihārena kira tvaṁ, sāriputta, etarahi bahulaṁ viharasi.
mn151:2.5.eng: It seems you usually practice the meditation of a great man.
mn151:2.5.comm: While the concept of a “great man” is familiar, the “meditation of a great man” (mahāpurisavihāra) appears only here and at Kd 22:2.5.10, which seems to borrow from this passage.

mn151:2.6.eng: For emptiness is the meditation of a great man.

mn151:3.2.pli: ‘suññatāvihārena bahulaṁ vihareyyan’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:
mn151:3.2.eng: ‘May I usually practice the meditation on emptiness.’ So they should reflect:

mn151:3.3.pli: ‘yena cāhaṁ maggena gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁ, yasmiñca padese piṇḍāya acariṁ, yena ca maggena gāmato piṇḍāya paṭikkamiṁ, atthi nu kho me tattha cakkhuviññeyyesu rūpesu chando vā rāgo vā doso vā moho vā paṭighaṁ vāpi cetaso’ti?
mn151:3.3.eng: ‘Along the path that I went for alms, or in the place I wandered for alms, or along the path that I returned from alms, was there any desire or greed or hate or delusion or repulsion in my heart for sights known by the eye?’
mn151:3.3.comm: Here the contemplation of the six sense fields is taught in the context of sense restraint through the active development of wisdom or discernment.

mn151:3.4.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:3.4.eng: Suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:3.5.pli: ‘yena cāhaṁ maggena gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁ, yasmiñca padese piṇḍāya acariṁ, yena ca maggena gāmato piṇḍāya paṭikkamiṁ, atthi me tattha cakkhuviññeyyesu rūpesu chando vā rāgo vā doso vā moho vā paṭighaṁ vāpi cetaso’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā tesaṁyeva pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya vāyamitabbaṁ.
mn151:3.5.eng: there was such desire or greed or hate or delusion or repulsion in their heart, they should make an effort to give up those unskillful qualities.

mn151:3.6.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:3.6.eng: But suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:3.7.pli: ‘yena cāhaṁ maggena gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁ, yasmiñca padese piṇḍāya acariṁ, yena ca maggena gāmato piṇḍāya paṭikkamiṁ, natthi me tattha cakkhuviññeyyesu rūpesu chando vā rāgo vā doso vā moho vā paṭighaṁ vāpi cetaso’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.
mn151:3.7.eng: there was no such desire or greed or hate or delusion or repulsion in their heart, they should meditate with rapture and joy, training day and night in skillful qualities.

mn151:4-8.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:4-8.2.pli: ‘yena cāhaṁ maggena gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁ, yasmiñca padese piṇḍāya acariṁ, yena ca maggena gāmato piṇḍāya paṭikkamiṁ, atthi nu kho me tattha sotaviññeyyesu saddesu …pe…
mn151:4-8.2.eng: ‘Along the path that I went for alms, or in the place I wandered for alms, or along the path that I returned from alms, was there any desire or greed or hate or delusion or repulsion in my heart for sounds known by the ear …

mn151:4-8.6.pli: manoviññeyyesu dhammesu chando vā rāgo vā doso vā moho vā paṭighaṁ vāpi cetaso’ti?

mn151:4-8.7.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:4-8.7.eng: Suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:4-8.8.pli: ‘yena cāhaṁ maggena gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁ, yasmiñca padese piṇḍāya acariṁ, yena ca maggena gāmato piṇḍāya paṭikkamiṁ, atthi me tattha manoviññeyyesu dhammesu chando vā rāgo vā doso vā moho vā paṭighaṁ vāpi cetaso’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā tesaṁyeva pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya vāyamitabbaṁ.
mn151:4-8.8.eng: there was such desire or greed or hate or delusion or repulsion in their heart, they should make an effort to give up those unskillful qualities.

mn151:4-8.9.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:4-8.9.eng: But suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:4-8.10.pli: ‘yena cāhaṁ maggena gāmaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁ, yasmiñca padese piṇḍāya acariṁ, yena ca maggena gāmato piṇḍāya paṭikkamiṁ, natthi me tattha manoviññeyyesu dhammesu chando vā rāgo vā doso vā moho vā paṭighaṁ vāpi cetaso’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.
mn151:4-8.10.eng: there was no such desire or greed or hate or delusion or repulsion in their heart, they should meditate with rapture and joy, training day and night in skillful qualities.

mn151:9.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:
mn151:9.1.comm: The following sections may be a later addition, as they are absent from the parallel (SA 236 at T ii 57b24).

mn151:9.2.eng: ‘Have I given up the five kinds of sensual stimulation?’

mn151:9.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:9.3.eng: Suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:9.4.pli: ‘appahīnā kho me pañca kāmaguṇā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā pañcannaṁ kāmaguṇānaṁ pahānāya vāyamitabbaṁ.
mn151:9.4.eng: they have not given them up, they should make an effort to do so.

mn151:9.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:9.5.eng: But suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:9.6.pli: ‘pahīnā kho me pañca kāmaguṇā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.
mn151:9.6.eng: they have given them up, they should meditate with rapture and joy, training day and night in skillful qualities.

mn151:10.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:10.2.eng: ‘Have I given up the five hindrances?’

mn151:10.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:10.3.eng: Suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:10.4.pli: ‘appahīnā kho me pañca nīvaraṇā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā pañcannaṁ nīvaraṇānaṁ pahānāya vāyamitabbaṁ.
mn151:10.4.eng: they have not given them up, they should make an effort to do so.

mn151:10.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:10.5.eng: But suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:10.6.pli: ‘pahīnā kho me pañca nīvaraṇā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.
mn151:10.6.eng: they have given them up, they should meditate with rapture and joy, training day and night in skillful qualities.

mn151:11.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:11.2.eng: ‘Have I completely understood the five grasping aggregates?’

mn151:11.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:11.3.eng: Suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:11.4.pli: ‘apariññātā kho me pañcupādānakkhandhā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā pañcannaṁ upādānakkhandhānaṁ pariññāya vāyamitabbaṁ.
mn151:11.4.eng: they have not completely understood them, they should make an effort to do so.

mn151:11.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:11.5.eng: But suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:11.6.pli: ‘pariññātā kho me pañcupādānakkhandhā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.
mn151:11.6.eng: they have completely understood them, they should meditate with rapture and joy, training day and night in skillful qualities.

mn151:12.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:12.2.eng: ‘Have I developed the four kinds of mindfulness meditation?’

mn151:12.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:12.3.eng: Suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:12.4.pli: ‘abhāvitā kho me cattāro satipaṭṭhānā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvanāya vāyamitabbaṁ.
mn151:12.4.eng: they haven’t developed them, they should make an effort to do so.

mn151:12.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:12.5.eng: But suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:12.6.pli: ‘bhāvitā kho me cattāro satipaṭṭhānā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.
mn151:12.6.eng: they have developed them, they should meditate with rapture and joy, training day and night in skillful qualities.

mn151:13.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:13.2.eng: ‘Have I developed the four right efforts …

mn151:13.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:

mn151:13.4.pli: ‘abhāvitā kho me cattāro sammappadhānā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā catunnaṁ sammappadhānānaṁ bhāvanāya vāyamitabbaṁ.

mn151:13.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:

mn151:13.6.pli: ‘bhāvitā kho me cattāro sammappadhānā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.

mn151:14.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:14.2.eng: the four bases of psychic power …

mn151:14.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:

mn151:14.4.pli: ‘abhāvitā kho me cattāro iddhipādā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā catunnaṁ iddhipādānaṁ bhāvanāya vāyamitabbaṁ.

mn151:14.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:

mn151:14.6.pli: ‘bhāvitā kho me cattāro iddhipādā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.

mn151:15.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:15.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:

mn151:15.4.pli: ‘abhāvitāni kho me pañcindriyānī’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā pañcannaṁ indriyānaṁ bhāvanāya vāyamitabbaṁ.

mn151:15.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:

mn151:15.6.pli: ‘bhāvitāni kho me pañcindriyānī’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.

mn151:16.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:16.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:

mn151:16.4.pli: ‘abhāvitāni kho me pañca balānī’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā pañcannaṁ balānaṁ bhāvanāya vāyamitabbaṁ.

mn151:16.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:

mn151:16.6.pli: ‘bhāvitāni kho me pañca balānī’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.

mn151:17.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:17.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:

mn151:17.4.pli: ‘abhāvitā kho me satta bojjhaṅgā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā sattannaṁ bojjhaṅgānaṁ bhāvanāya vāyamitabbaṁ.

mn151:17.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:

mn151:17.6.pli: ‘bhāvitā kho me satta bojjhaṅgā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.

mn151:18.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:18.2.pli: ‘bhāvito nu kho me ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo’ti?

mn151:18.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:18.3.eng: Suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:18.4.pli: ‘abhāvito kho me ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa bhāvanāya vāyamitabbaṁ.
mn151:18.4.eng: they haven’t developed it, they should make an effort to do so.

mn151:18.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:18.5.eng: But suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:18.6.pli: ‘bhāvito kho me ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.
mn151:18.6.eng: they have developed it, they should meditate with rapture and joy, training day and night in skillful qualities.

mn151:19.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:19.2.pli: ‘bhāvitā nu kho me samatho ca vipassanā cā’ti?

mn151:19.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:19.3.eng: Suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:19.4.pli: ‘abhāvitā kho me samatho ca vipassanā cā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā samathavipassanānaṁ bhāvanāya vāyamitabbaṁ.
mn151:19.4.eng: they haven’t developed them, they should make an effort to do so.

mn151:19.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:19.5.eng: But suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:19.6.pli: ‘bhāvitā kho me samatho ca vipassanā cā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.
mn151:19.6.eng: they have developed them, they should meditate with rapture and joy, training day and night in skillful qualities.

mn151:20.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, sāriputta, bhikkhunā iti paṭisañcikkhitabbaṁ:

mn151:20.2.pli: ‘sacchikatā nu kho me vijjā ca vimutti cā’ti?

mn151:20.3.pli: Sace, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:20.3.eng: Suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:20.4.pli: ‘asacchikatā kho me vijjā ca vimutti cā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā vijjāya vimuttiyā sacchikiriyāya vāyamitabbaṁ.
mn151:20.4.eng: they haven’t realized them, they should make an effort to do so.

mn151:20.5.pli: Sace pana, sāriputta, bhikkhu paccavekkhamāno evaṁ jānāti:
mn151:20.5.eng: But suppose that, upon checking, a mendicant knows that

mn151:20.6.pli: ‘sacchikatā kho me vijjā ca vimutti cā’ti, tena, sāriputta, bhikkhunā teneva pītipāmojjena vihātabbaṁ ahorattānusikkhinā kusalesu dhammesu.
mn151:20.6.eng: they have realized them, they should meditate with rapture and joy, training day and night in skillful qualities.
mn151:20.6.comm: Even the arahant continues to meditate and practice since it is a happy way to live, and to set a good example.

mn151:21.1.pli: Ye hi keci, sāriputta, atītamaddhānaṁ samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā piṇḍapātaṁ parisodhesuṁ, sabbe te evameva paccavekkhitvā paccavekkhitvā piṇḍapātaṁ parisodhesuṁ.
mn151:21.1.eng: Whether in the past, future, or present, all those who purify their almsfood do so by continually checking in this way.

mn151:21.2.pli: Yepi hi keci, sāriputta, anāgatamaddhānaṁ samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā piṇḍapātaṁ parisodhessanti, sabbe te evameva paccavekkhitvā paccavekkhitvā piṇḍapātaṁ parisodhessanti.

mn151:21.3.pli: Yepi hi keci, sāriputta, etarahi samaṇā vā brāhmaṇā vā piṇḍapātaṁ parisodhenti, sabbe te evameva paccavekkhitvā paccavekkhitvā piṇḍapātaṁ parisodhenti.

mn151:21.4.eng: So, Sāriputta, you should all train like this: ‘We shall purify our almsfood by continually checking.’”
mn151:21.4.comm: This clarifies the overall purpose of the discourse, to set out a method for mendicants to reflect during their daily walk for almsfood, at which time they are likely to encounter distracting sights, sounds, and smells.

mn151:21.6.eng: That is what the Buddha said.

mn151:21.7.pli: Attamano āyasmā sāriputto bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinandīti.
mn151:21.7.eng: Satisfied, Venerable Sāriputta approved what the Buddha said.
